item.title: Fed’s Bowman Says Too Soon to Judge Inflation Impact of Iran War
item.description: Federal Reserve Vice Chair for Supervision Michelle Bowman said it’s too soon to judge the inflationary impact from the Iran war and that policymakers need to look through temporary price shocks.

item.title: Zambia Bonds Jump After 2053 Debt Buyback Tender Starts
item.description: Zambia launched a cash tender offer to buy back $1.36 billion bond due in 2053, becoming the latest African nation to repurchase outstanding debt as governments seek to reduce their servicing costs.

item.title: US Merchandise-Trade Deficit Narrows on Rise in Exports
item.description: The US merchandise-trade deficit narrowed in April, reflecting an increase in the value of exports of capital and consumer goods.

item.title: Canada Dips Into Technical Recession for First Time Since 2020
item.description: The Canadian economy edged into a technical recession as weak business and government spending drove a slight contraction in the first quarter.

item.title: ‘Two Sigma’ S&P Rally Signals Easy Money Has Already Been Made
item.description: The S&P 500 Index has returned to all-time highs, yet there’s no mistaking that Wall Street’s breathtaking two-month rally has slowed dramatically.

item.title: India’s Maritime Financier Readies Nation’s First Blue Bond
item.description: Sagarmala Finance Corp., a financier created to fund India’s maritime projects, is preparing to issue the country’s maiden blue bond to raise as much as 10 billion rupees ($105 million), according to a top official.

item.title: Brazil’s Economy Rebounds as Lula Cranks Up Aid Before Election
item.description: Brazil’s economy picked up more than expected at the start of the year, powered by government stimulus and robust mining that fueled growth in the face of double-digit borrowing costs.

item.title: Unknown MAGA Warrior Upends Race for a Seat on Texas Oil Regulator
item.description: Hard-right candidate with little connection to the oil industry is primed to win a place on the Railroad Commission of Texas.

item.title: Danish Pension Fund to Blacklist ‘Grossly Overvalued’ SpaceX
item.description: A Danish pension fund that earlier this year made headlines by ditching Treasuries as Donald Trump was threatening to seize Greenland now says it won’t touch SpaceX.

item.title: US Premarket Movers for May 29, 2026
item.description: S&P 500 Index futures rise 0.1% as of 7:31 a.m. in New York as traders wait to see whether the US and Iran can finally get a peace deal across the line.

item.title: Hershey Bull Sees World Cup, America 250 Lifting Chocolate Sales
item.description: Summer events including the FIFA World Cup and America’s 250th anniversary could spur sales of sweet treats, making now a key time to snap up beaten-down shares of Hershey Co., according to the candy and snack maker’s newest Wall Street bull.

item.title: Czech Billionaire Sees Potential to Boost TotalEnergies Stake
item.description: Czech billionaire Daniel Kretinsky said he’s open to increasing his holding in TotalEnergies SE, after gaining a stake in the French oil major last month.

item.title: Intesa Seals SRTs on $4.8 Billion in US Corporate and ESG Loans
item.description: Intesa Sanpaolo SpA has wrapped up two significant risk transfers tied to corporate loans worth about $4.8 billion as it takes advantage of robust demand to make greater use of the hedging tool.

item.title: Aluminum Squeeze Deepens as Spreads Tighten and Inventories Drop
item.description: The aluminum market is facing a deepening shortage as the closure of the Strait of Hormuz throttles supply, with spot prices for the metal spiking while exchange inventories slump.

item.title: AI Used to Be Generative. Now It’s All About Agents
item.description: Corporate speak has changed when it comes to describing the evolving technology

item.title: Grocery Shoppers Are In For a Summer of Pain
item.description: The Iran war’s effects will keep rippling through the supply chain, even if it ends.

item.title: The Debut Bessent-Warsh Breakfast Left Fed Rate Cuts Off the Menu
item.description: New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh may have a couple of months before any White House pressure to cut rates.

item.title: Zurich Bank Looks to Brazil, Florida as It Expands in Americas
item.description: EFG International AG, a private-banking boutique based in Zurich, is expanding its 300-person team serving clients from the Americas with an emphasis on Brazil and South Florida.

item.title: IndiGo Posts Unexpected Loss as Mideast Turmoil Lifts Costs
item.description: IndiGo, Asia’s largest low-cost carrier, reported an unexpected quarterly loss, amid cost pressures, operational disruptions and softer demand linked to the Middle East conflict.

item.title: Indonesia and India Intervene to Prop Up Weakening Currencies
item.description: Indonesia and India intervened on Friday to prop up their currencies, which are being pressured by an energy price spike.

item.title: It’s Summertime and AI Means Internships Aren’t Easy
item.description: With chatbots like Claude around, more companies are cutting back on internships.

item.title: Fed’s Schmid Says Officials Must Signal Commitment to Inflation
item.description: Federal Reserve Bank of Kansas City President Jeff Schmid said he remained focused on elevated inflation and officials should make it clear they’re willing to do what is needed to achieve price stability.

item.title: Sell in May Proves to Be a Losing Trade in US Stocks
item.description: S&P 500 set to gain for a ninth straight week as AI trade powers on.

item.title: Italy’s Growth Was Robust at Start of 2026 in Surprise Revision
item.description: Italy’s economy grew more than initially estimated at the start of 2026, keeping up momentum from last year before the shock of the Iran war impacted growth.

item.title: Japan Used Record $73.6 Billion to Support Yen in Past Month
item.description: Japan used the equivalent of a record ¥11.73 trillion ($73.6 billion) over the past month to support the yen after the currency slid past 160 per dollar, according to Finance Ministry data confirming the government’s first market intervention since 2024.

item.title: The Nightmare of Finding a Summer Internship in the Age of AI
item.description: An important rite of passage for college students is slipping away as companies use AI for more busywork and prepare for a future with fewer entry-level positions.

item.title: Bank of England May Tolerate Inflation to Support UK Economy, Bailey Says
item.description: The Bank of England could tolerate inflation temporarily staying above its 2% target in order to support the UK’s weak economy, Governor Andrew Bailey said — so long as second-round price effects do not emerge.

item.title: Key Japanese Aluminum Fee Set for Record as War Squeezes Supply
item.description: Rio Tinto Group and South32 Ltd. have offered Japanese clients third-quarter aluminum supply at record premiums, said traders familiar with the matter, after conflict in the Middle East tightened the global market.

item.title: Spain Urges Billions for EU Farmers to Ease Fertilizer Shock
item.description: The European Union will need to provide farmers with several billion euros of funding to prevent crop cutbacks following a surge in fertilizer costs caused by the Iran war, Spain Agriculture Minister Luis Planas said.

item.title: India RBI Paid Record Dividend From Currency Gains, Report Says
item.description: The Reserve Bank of India paid a record dividend to the government after higher interest income and large gains from foreign exchange transactions boosted earnings, the central bank’s annual report showed.
